title: Noise Modulation
subtitle: Interpretability and robustness study
summary: An unpublished paper proposing a lightweight way to recover cleaner, more interpretable input gradients without full adversarial training.

blocks.text: The idea was to multiply images with learned noise during training. That pushed the model to rely on the truly critical components, cleaning up the gradient signal at the cost of some accuracy.
The paper will never be published, it's archived here:

items: [Noise Modulation: Let your model interpret itself](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.10603)
